\paragraph{Canonical Equation.}
$$ DECLARE input_x FLOAT8; input_y FLOAT8; input_z FLOAT8; lat FLOAT8; lon FLOAT8; best_index INTEGER; best_dot FLOAT8 := -2.0; current_dot FLOAT8; result daansphere_point; BEGIN -- Extract lat/lon from PostGIS geography lat := ST_Y(point_geog::geometry); lon := ST_X(point_geog::geometry); -- Convert to Cartesian (this is what PostGIS does internally anyway) input_x := cos(radians(lat)) * cos(radians(lon)); input_y := cos(radians(lat)) * sin(radians(lon)); input_z := sin(radians(lat)); -- Find nearest DAANSphere point using dot product -- This is the key optimization - no trigonometry needed! SELECT sphere_index, (x * input_x + y * input_y + z * input_z) as dot_product INTO best_index, best_ ...
